Fixed now.

Patch Set 27: Code-Review-1

Hi,

> For usability, does it make sense to use the nodemap subdirectory mountpoint as the default template FID, if it is not otherwise specified?  That would make subdirectory mounts behave more like a full filesystem mount, where setstripe on the root directory becomes the default for the whole filesystem.

Yes, I think it makes a lot of sense.

Etienne confirmed to me that this approach does not work out of the box yet, but we agree it is an option we should seriously consider.
Etienne is currently working on LU-10262 (ie. improving open()'s performance with O_CREAT when a file already exists).
But this is the next item on our todolist.
As soon as we confirm this is viable, we will abandon this patch.

We tried to figure out how this would work, can you tell us if this is what you have in mind?

The use case for LU-9982 is to be able to have a different default striping depending on which lustre client one uses.
The idea being that different client nodes may have affinities with different OST pools.

The way we would do this with subdir mount + implicit default root striping would be to initialize our FS like this:

lustre
└── template-0
    └── template-1
        └── template-2
            └── ...
                └── template-n
                    ├── user-0
                    ├── user-1
                    ├── user-2
                    └── user-n

Where template-0, template-1, ..., template-n are directories with explicit stripings that relate to the different client affinities we have on our cluster.

Then, depending on the client, we would "subdir mount" either template-0, template-1, ..., or template-n, and then do a bind mount or a symlink to template-n and expose this as our "real FS" to users.

Files created without an explicit striping would inherit the default template-X that is currently mounted on the client, which does satisfy our use case.

The major issue for this patch is to get template default layout information for directories on the client side.

Before LU-11739 the information of the default layout was always contained in directories. Currently, the client have to determine by himself what is the default layout to use between :
	- 1) directory layout
	- 2) root layout

(cf. LU-11739, LU-11656 Jian Yu's comment, https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/36609/11/lustre/llite/dir.c)

This patch add a default layout that is dynamic in function of the client, so we can not do the same thing. The client have to retrieve the template nodemap information to select the good template to use.
But maybe, it is easier to modify getattr server side to link to the correct layout.

To sum up, I need some guidance to continue because I don't known how to do it.

Here the current state of the patch:
 - For lov layout it is working well except that the information display by "lfs get*stripe" on directories are wrong if a template default layout is applicable.
 - For lmv layout the patch is not working. If the template default layout is applicable on a directory, the client will always "guess" that the default layout is "root". Then at file creation, the client will determine which MDT to "talk" to, depending on the root layout. If the template layout differs from the root layout then the client will request file creation on the wrong MDT and the MDT will returns EREMOTE.

Patch Set 28:

> The major issue for this patch is to get template default
 > layout information for directories on the client side.

Etienne, the ll_dir_getstripe_default() and ll_dir_get_default_layout() functions are only used for "lfs getstripe", they are not used when creating a new file/directory on the MDS.

Even so, within the context of ll_dir_getstripe_default(), it would be possible to change the MDS code to get the default layout from the root of the *fileset*, rather than the root of the filesystem by having it check for { FID_SEQ_ROOT, FID_OID_ROOT, 0 } and map that to the template FID rather than the actual ROOT/ inode.

Since the MDS is the one that knows whether the client has mounted a fileset or not, it is in a position to return the correct information.  It would also be possible to use sbi->ll_root_fid instead of lu_root_fid() in ll_dir_get_default_layout() for the GET_DEFAULT_LAYOUT_ROOT case, but the client doesn't know if there is a template FID for the whole fileset or not, and the template is not required to match the fileset root (e.g. if the admin wants to provide a different template that is outside the fileset).

commit.subject: LU-9982 lustre: Clients striping from mapped FID in nodemap
commit.message:
LU-9982 lustre: Clients striping from mapped FID in nodemap

New feature in nodemap: you can now define a FID as template layout
in a nodemap. It can be specified by FID or path.

Example of use:
- create a file (or directory) in lustre fs with some explicit
striping:
 lfs setstripe -c 4 /mnt/lustre/template
or
 lfs setdirstripe -D --mdt-index 2 2 /mnt/lustre/template

lctl nodemap_add  SG1
lctl nodemap_add_range --name SG1 --range my_range
lctl nodemap_set_template --name SG1 --template 0x200000402:0x1:0x0
or
nodemap_set_template --name SG1 --template "[0x200000402:0x1:0x0]"
or
nodemap_set_template --name SG1 --template /mnt/lustre/template

Clients in the nodemap SG1 will use the template as default layout.

But it does not break Lustre layout philosophy, the layout rules
are:
1. Explicit layout prevails (lfs setstripe / setdirstripe)
2. Append layout prevails (if O_APPEND flag is set at creation:
   see append_stripe_count and append_pool mdd parameters)
3. Parent layout prevails
4. Template layout
5. Root layout
6. FS default (define with config parameters
   ex: .lov.*.mdtlov.stripecount)

Performances impact compared to master (file creation):
- Patch with feature disabled: no impact
- Patch with feature enabled:  +2%

TODO:
	- get the correct default layout "read" by the client: for LMV
	  layout (client have to know on which MDT the md object is
	  located ) and for lfs getstripe or lfs getdirstripe.
	  (cf. LU-11656)
	- Tests
	- Documentation
